============================

── Example 11: Sales Star Schema (End-to-End Query) ────────────
  SQL:
    SELECT d_dim_product.category, d_dim_customer.city, SUM(f.revenue) AS sum_revenue, COUNT(*) AS count
    FROM fact_sales f
    LEFT JOIN dim_product d_dim_product ON f.product_sk = d_dim_product.id AND d_dim_product.is_current = TRUE
    LEFT JOIN dim_customer d_dim_customer ON f.customer_sk = d_dim_customer.id AND d_dim_customer.is_current = TRUE
    GROUP BY d_dim_product.category, d_dim_customer.city

  Results (category × city → revenue, orders):
  Category       City                    Revenue  Orders
  -------------- -------------------- ----------  ------
  consumables    New Jamesside        $   363.43       1
  tools          Franciscostad        $   113.47       1
  widgets        East Lydiamouth      $   472.56       2

── Example 12: Point-in-Time Price Query (AS OF 2023-12-31) ────
  AS OF 2023-12-31 (historical prices):
    product 1  Purpose Pro                 list=$52.74  revenue=$118.98
    product 2  Brother                     list=$136.95  revenue=$399.16
    product 3  Ago Plus                    list=$87.26  revenue=$382.63
    product 4  Site                        list=$50.36  revenue=$263.34
    product 5  Face Lite                   list=$86.80  revenue=$339.11
    product 6  Election Ultra              list=$152.96  revenue=$73.40

  AS OF today (current prices):
    product 1  Purpose Pro v2              list=$57.48  revenue=$113.47
    product 2  Brother                     list=$136.95  revenue=$399.16
    product 3  Ago Plus                    list=$100.84  revenue=$363.43
    product 6  Election Ultra              list=$152.96  revenue=$73.40

  Note: versioned products appear in BOTH views but at different prices.
        AS OF 2023-12-31 → original price (lower);
        current view     → post-update price — correct SCD2 AS OF.

Done.

Fact Patterns Showcase
======================

── Example 5: E-commerce Orders (Accumulating Snapshot) ────────
  T1 placed    → {'inserted': 6, 'updated': 0}
  T2 paid      → {'inserted': 0, 'updated': 4}
  T3 shipped   → {'inserted': 0, 'updated': 3}
  T4 delivered → {'inserted': 0, 'updated': 1}
  Final pipeline state:
    order 1001: placed → paid → shipped → delivered  ✓ COMPLETE
    order 1002: placed → paid → shipped  (pending: delivered)
    order 1003: placed → paid → shipped  (pending: delivered)
    order 1004: placed → paid  (pending: shipped, delivered)
    order 1005: placed  (pending: paid, shipped, delivered)
    order 1006: placed  (pending: paid, shipped, delivered)

── Example 6: Daily Account Balances (Periodic Snapshot) ───────
  2024-03-29 → 5 rows loaded
  2024-03-30 → 5 rows loaded
  2024-03-31 → 5 rows loaded
  Total rows: 15 (5 accounts × 3 days)
  March 31 snapshot:
    account 1001  (checking    )  $  4,068.67  @ 2024-03-31
    account 1002  (money_market)  $ 37,361.51  @ 2024-03-31
    account 1003  (savings     )  $109,477.07  @ 2024-03-31
    account 1004  (checking    )  $ 86,843.20  @ 2024-03-31
    account 1005  (checking    )  $  4,841.40  @ 2024-03-31

── Example 7: NBA Player Seasons (Cumulative Fact) ─────────────
  → Full implementation in examples/real_world/nba_analytics/
    Key class: CumulativeMixin — appends one JSON struct per season

── Example 8: User Daily Activity (Bitmask Loader) ─────────────
  → Full implementation in examples/real_world/user_activity/
    Key class: LazyBitmaskLoader / BitmaskerLoader
    32-bit integer encodes up to 28 days; L7/L28 = bit-shift test

Done.

── Example 15: Custom Sink — MotherDuck Pattern ────────────────
  isinstance(MotherDuckSink(...), SinkAdapter) … False
  SCD2 via MotherDuckSink → inserted=2
  MotherDuckSink implements all 6 SinkAdapter methods:
    ✓ current_state_sql()
    ✓ write()
    ✓ close_versions()
    ✓ update_attributes()
    ✓ rotate_attributes()
    ✓ update_milestones()

  To target MotherDuck replace db= with your database name and
  pass token=os.environ['MOTHERDUCK_TOKEN'].

Done.

============================================================
  sqldim Hierarchy Dimensions — Three Strategy Showcase
============================================================

── AdjacencyListStrategy ──────────────────────────────────────

  Ancestors of Grace (id=7):
    id=4  parent=2  depth=1
    id=2  parent=1  depth=2
    id=1  parent=None  depth=3

  Descendants of Bob (id=2):
    id=4  parent=2  depth=1
    id=5  parent=2  depth=1
    id=7  parent=4  depth=2

  Rollup revenue to VP level (depth=1):
    ancestor_id=2  revenue_sum=14,700.0
    ancestor_id=3  revenue_sum=12,300.0

── MaterializedPathStrategy ───────────────────────────────────

  Ancestors of Grace (id=7) via path LIKE:
    Alice (CEO)  (path=/1/)
    Bob (VP Eng)  (path=/1/2/)
    Dave (SWE)  (path=/1/2/4/)

  Descendants of Alice (id=1) via path LIKE:
    Bob (VP Eng)  (path=/1/2/)
    Carol (VP Sales)  (path=/1/3/)
    Dave (SWE)  (path=/1/2/4/)
    Eve (SWE)  (path=/1/2/5/)
    Frank (AE)  (path=/1/3/6/)
    Grace (Intern)  (path=/1/2/4/7/)

  build_path('/1/2/', 8) → '/1/2/8/'

── ClosureTableStrategy ───────────────────────────────────────

  Ancestors of Grace (id=7) via closure join:
    id=4  Dave (SWE)
    id=2  Bob (VP Eng)
    id=1  Alice (CEO)

  Descendants of Bob (id=2) via closure join:
    id=4  Dave (SWE)
    id=5  Eve (SWE)
    id=7  Grace (Intern)

  Rollup revenue to VP level (depth=1) via closure:
    ancestor_id=2  revenue_sum=14,700.0
    ancestor_id=3  revenue_sum=12,300.0

── HierarchyRoller (strategy-agnostic) ────────────────────────

  Revenue rolled to VP level via HierarchyRoller:
    Bob (VP Eng)          $14,700.0
    Carol (VP Sales)      $12,300.0

── HierarchyMixin class API ────────────────────────────────────

  OrgDimModel.ancestors_sql(7) → 3 ancestors found
  OrgDimModel.descendants_sql(1) → 6 descendants of Alice

  ClosureTableStrategy.build_closure_sql: length=529 chars

🏀 Initializing sqldim Showcase: Michael Jordan Career Data

✅ Pillar 1: Cumulative snapshots built for Michael Jordan
   Stored dense history in 'player' table (1993, 1995, 1998).

✅ Pillar 2: SCD Type 2 timeline generated via backfill_scd2()
   Found 3 historical versions in 'player_scd' table:
     - 1993 to 1993: ScoringClass.star (Active)
     - 1995 to 1995: ScoringClass.average (Retired)
     - 1998 to 1998: ScoringClass.star (Active)

✅ Pillar 3: Semantic 'AS OF' Query (Time Travel)
✅ Pillar 4: Graph Projection (Dual-Paradigm)
   Vertex 'Michael Jordan' played Game ID: 2323
   Path found via Recursive CTE projection.

🚀 Showcase complete. sqldim: Kimball methodology meets Graph power.

☁️ CloudStream SaaS Showcase: Viral Growth & Retention

✅ Feature: Vectorized ETL (Narwhals)
   Processed batch of 2 users using single-pass joins.

✅ Feature: Dual-Paradigm Graph
   User 'alice@cloud.com' found 1 referral(s) via Recursive CTE.
   First Referral: bob@cloud.com (Plan: pro)

✅ Feature: Bitmask Retention (L7/L28)
   User 'bob@cloud.com' L7 Engagement Score: 5 / 7 days
   Stored 5 active days in a single integer-encoded field.

🚀 SaaS Growth Pipeline complete. Fully Dimensional. Fully Networked.

🛒 E-Commerce Order Intelligence Pipeline

   Loaded 5 customers via CustomersSource factory.
   Total version rows in dim_customer: 5 (0 expired / upgraded).
     blakeerik@example.com            tier=platinum     (current)

     garzaanthony@example.org         tier=bronze       (current)

     johnsonjoshua@example.org        tier=bronze       (current)

     ORD-00099  stage=placed        qty=1  value=$81.37
     ORD-00131  stage=placed        qty=5  value=$1452.45

   No schema drift detected — schema is up to date.

🚀 E-Commerce Pipeline complete. SCD2 + Bridge + Accumulating + Migrations.

🏦 Fintech Payment Intelligence Pipeline

✅ Pillar 1: SCD Type 2 — Account Risk-Tier History
   Loaded 3 accounts via AccountsSource factory.
   Total version rows in dim_account: 4 (1 expired / escalated).
     Victoria Wyatt      risk=high     (current)

     Ian Cooper          risk=medium   (current)

     account_id=3  month=2024-05-01  closing=$8,190.00  trend=↓

📦 Supply Chain Inventory Intelligence Pipeline

✅ Pillar 1: SCD Type 2 — Warehouse Capacity History
   Loaded 3 warehouses via WarehousesSource factory.
   Total version rows in dim_warehouse: 4 (1 expired / capacity-changed).
     Cassandraton DC       capacity=154,194  (current)
     New Jamesside DC      capacity= 68,369  (current)
     South Joshuastad DC   capacity= 83,158  (expanded)
     South Joshuastad DC   capacity=115,809  (current)

✅ Pillar 2: Shipment Graph — Hub-and-Spoke Routing
   New Jamesside DC is the consolidation hub (from WarehousesSource factory).
   Outbound destinations: ['Cassandraton DC', 'South Joshuastad DC']
   Total units shipped: 13,000

✅ Pillar 3: Cumulative Fact — Running Stock-Level Arrays
     warehouse_id=1  stock=20,000  reserved=5,000  available=15,000
     warehouse_id=4  stock=12,000  reserved=3,000  available= 9,000
   Total available across network: 24,000 units

✅ Pillar 4: Schema Graph — Dimensional Lineage Diagram
   SchemaGraph built: 3 dimensions, 3 fact/edge tables.
   Mermaid diagram: 56 lines
   (Use SchemaGraph.to_mermaid() to render in any Markdown viewer)

✅ Pillar 5: DGM Graph Analytics (v0.11 features)
   TrimJoin(SINK_FREE): TrimJoin(<join>, SINK_FREE())
   TARJAN_SCC node expr: community_label(tarjan_scc()) /* alias=w */
   DEGREE(OUT) hub expr: degree(direction='OUT') /* alias=w */
   ArithExpr quantity×cost: (sh.quantity * sh.unit_cost)
   SubgraphExpr(DENSITY, partition=[w.region]): density() /* partition=[w.region] */

🚀 Supply Chain Pipeline complete. SCD2 + Shipment Graph + Cumulative Inventory + Schema Graph + DGM Analytics.

🖱️ Initializing User Activity Showcase: Bitmask Retention

✅ Pillar 1: User activity accumulated for User 101.
   Dates active: 10 unique days stored in a list.

✅ Pillar 2: Activity encoded into 32-bit integer.
   Integer Value: 4263577600
   Binary Representation: 0b11111110001000010000100000000000

✅ Pillar 3: Retention Metrics (via bitwise ops)
   Is Active Today?        : True
   L7 (Active days last 7) : 7
   L28 (Active days last 28): 10
   Active in last 30 days? : True

🚀 Showcase complete. From raw lists to high-performance bitmask analytics.
